title: Master_Recycling_Guide_Part_6_Electronics_Batteries_Hhw
text:
Rules vary by state and locality. Follow local guidance.

### Can I recycle fluorescent light bulbs?

Use a household hazardous waste or retailer collection program.

### Can I recycle motor oil?

Motor oil is commonly accepted through automotive recycling or HHW
programs.

### Can I recycle paint?

Latex paint disposal varies by state. Oil-based paint is typically HHW.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Common Mistakes

Do not place these in curbside recycling:

-   Loose batteries
-   Phones
-   Computers
-   TVs
-   Paint
-   Chemicals
-   Syringes
-   Propane cylinders
-   Fire extinguishers

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Search Synonyms

Users may search for:

-   E-waste
-   Electronic waste
-   Computer
-   Laptop
-   Phone
-   Cell phone
-   Battery
-   Lithium battery
-   Rechargeable battery
-   Car battery
-   Paint
-   Motor oil
-   Antifreeze
-   CFL bulb
-   Fluorescent tube
-   Ink cartridge
-   Toner cartridge

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Best Practices

-   Use certified e-waste recyclers.
-   Never mix hazardous waste with household recycling.
-   Store batteries safely until recycling.
-   Check local HHW collection events.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

-   U.S. EPA Electronics Donation and Recycling:
    https://www.epa.gov/recycle/electronics-donation-and-recycling
-   U.S. EPA Household Hazardous Waste:

-   Call2Recycle: https://www.call2recycle.org/
-   Earth911 Recycling Search: https://search.earth911.com/
